subject: Computer Networking
title: Incident Response Plans
description: A quick win: understand Incident Response Plans.

hook.text: A breach is discovered at 2 AM. Who do you call? What do you shut down? Without a plan, panic takes over.

buildup.text: An incident response plan defines roles, communication chains, and steps for containment, eradication, and recovery.

discovery.text: Tabletop exercises simulate breaches. Teams practice decisions risk-free so they're ready when it counts.

twist.text: Companies that rehearse respond faster and lose less. Those without plans average 287 days to detect a breach.

climax.text: After containment, a post-mortem identifies root causes and improves defenses. Every incident is a lesson.

punchline.text: A plan you practice beats a plan that gathers dust.

quiz.question: What is a tabletop exercise in incident response?
options: A simulated breach scenario to practice response decisions
A physical inspection of server hardware
A method of encrypting backup tapes
